government_hackathons.full_name: iDEX Open Challenge 2026 - Innovations for Defence Excellence
government_hackathons.hosting_organization: Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO), Ministry of Defence
problem_statements: Open challenge - innovators define their own problem statement relevant to defence and aerospace applications

eligibility_criteria.summary: DPIIT-recognized startups; MSMEs as per MSME Act 2006; individual innovators; academic/research institutions. All must be Indian entities with solutions applicable to defence and aerospace.

prizes.summary: Up to INR 1.5 crore grant via SPARK framework; potential follow-on funding and defence procurement pathway

government_hackathons.full_name: ZEROBREACH CTF 2026 - National Level Cybersecurity Competition
problem_statements: Jeopardy-style CTF challenges across multiple cybersecurity domains

eligibility_criteria.summary: Open to both beginners and professionals; solo or team of up to 2 members

prizes.summary: Prize pool available for top performers

government_hackathons.full_name: D.E.S.I.G.N for BioE3 Challenge - Empowering Youth to Solve Critical Issues of their TIMES
government_hackathons.theme: Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment
problem_statements: Category 1 (School students Class VI-XII): innovative biotechnological solutions to address real-world challenges using microbes, molecules, and systems biology; Category 2 (All Indian citizens): scalable biotechnological innovations
government_hackathons.application_deadline: Monthly cycles - 20th of each month (next: May 20, 2026)

eligibility_criteria.summary: Category 1: Students of classes VI-XII from any Indian school; Category 2: Any Indian citizen. Teams of up to 5 members from same school for Category 1.

prizes.summary: Category 1: 10 Certificates of Merit per cycle; Category 2: Rs. 1 Lakh cash prize for top 10 solutions + option to apply for Rs. 25 Lakh BIRAC RDI grant

government_hackathons.full_name: Blockchain India Challenge - Citizen Centric Blockchain and Web3 Solutions
government_hackathons.theme: Non-crypto based blockchain and Web3 applications for digital governance
problem_statements: Four rounds of competition (Ideation, Prototype, MVP, Deployment) focused on non-crypto blockchain applications for government use cases across land administration, supply chain, health data, e-procurement, and document management

eligibility_criteria.summary: Startups registered in India; at least 51% Indian ownership; all members must be Indian citizens aged 18+; DPIIT recognition not required at application but needed for prototype stage

prizes.summary: Total prize pool INR 8.80 Crore; up to INR 60 Lakhs for Prototype stage; up to INR 1 Crore for MVP stage; INR 50 Lakhs for Grand Winners (10 winners from 10 categories)

official_open_keywords_found: Start-ups are requested to sign up immediately

eligibility_criteria.summary: Participants invited via CSIR/MyGov listing; specific eligibility not enumerated on the listing excerpt.
